questions.question: Why is agriculture considered a primary activity?
questions.answer: Because it directly uses natural resources to produce food and raw materials.

questions.question: Why does primitive subsistence farming depend on monsoon?
questions.answer: Because it uses little irrigation and depends mainly on rainfall.

questions.question: Why is intensive subsistence farming practiced in densely populated regions?
questions.answer: Because farmers try to produce maximum output from small land areas.

questions.question: Why is commercial farming different from subsistence farming?
questions.answer: Commercial farming produces crops mainly for sale in markets.

questions.question: Why are transport facilities important for plantation agriculture?
questions.answer: They help transport crops to industries and markets.

questions.question: Why do Rabi crops require irrigation in many regions?
questions.answer: Because rainfall is low during winter.

questions.question: Why do Kharif crops depend on monsoon rainfall?
questions.answer: Because they are grown during the rainy season.

questions.question: Why is rice grown mainly in high rainfall areas?
questions.answer: Because rice requires abundant water and humidity.

questions.question: Why is wheat mainly grown in northern plains?
questions.answer: Because the region provides suitable cool climate and fertile soil.

questions.question: Why are millets suitable for dry areas?
questions.answer: Because they require less rainfall.

questions.question: Why are pulses important for soil fertility?
questions.answer: Because they fix nitrogen in the soil.

questions.question: Why is sugarcane called a commercial crop?
questions.answer: Because it is used as raw material for sugar industries.

questions.question: Why are oilseeds important in India?
questions.answer: Because they produce edible oils used in cooking.

questions.question: Why is tea cultivation labour intensive?
questions.answer: Because tea leaves are picked manually.

questions.question: Why is coffee mainly grown in southern hill regions?
questions.answer: Because the climate and soil conditions are suitable there.

questions.question: Why are horticulture crops economically valuable?
questions.answer: Because fruits and vegetables have high market demand.

questions.question: Why does cotton grow well in black soil?
questions.answer: Because black soil retains moisture and nutrients.

questions.question: Why is jute grown mainly in flood plains?
questions.answer: Because flood plains provide fertile soil and water.

questions.question: Why does rubber require a hot and humid climate?
questions.answer: Because rubber trees grow best in such conditions.

questions.question: Why is irrigation important for agriculture?
questions.answer: Because it provides water when rainfall is insufficient.

questions.question: Why were technological reforms introduced in agriculture?
questions.answer: To increase agricultural productivity.

questions.question: Why was the Green Revolution important?
questions.answer: It increased food grain production in India.

questions.question: Why do farmers need government support?
questions.answer: To get financial help and protection for their crops.

questions.question: Why do cropping patterns vary in different regions?
questions.answer: Because climate and soil conditions differ across regions.

questions.question: Why is sustainable agriculture important?
questions.answer: Because it protects natural resources and ensures long-term food production.
